outline.title: Introduction and Background
outline.objective: Establish the research problem of automating literature review generation and position the current review within the broader NLP landscape

outline.title: Methodological Approaches to Automated Literature Review Generation
outline.objective: Categorize and compare the three main approaches: extractive summarization (spaCy), abstractive summarization (T5), and retrieval-augmented generation (GPT-3.5)

outline.title: Performance Evaluation and Metric Analysis
outline.objective: Synthesize ROUGE-based evaluation results across methods and examine claim conflicts regarding method superiority

outline.title: Datasets and Generalizability Constraints
outline.objective: Analyze the reliance on SciTLDR dataset and its implications for domain applicability and cross-disciplinary transfer

outline.title: Language and Geographic Representation Gaps
outline.objective: Identify the underrepresentation of Chinese-language studies and discuss implications for cross-lingual literature review automation

outline.title: Open Challenges and Future Research Directions
outline.objective: Consolidate unresolved claim differences, methodological limitations, and propose a framework for standardized comparison in future work

sections.title: Introduction and Background
sections.text: This review synthesizes evidence from 1 papers addressing text_summarization; multi_document_summarization; automated_literature_review_generation, drawn from the automated literature review domain. Systems examined employ diverse methodological families, including extractive_summarization; abstractive_summarization; retrieval_augmented_generation. The review structures findings around 4 discrete claims, organized into a comparative matrix that highlights where methods converge, diverge, or lack sufficient evaluation evidence. The objective is to move beyond siloed paper summaries toward a structured understanding of the current state, open problems, and underexplored regions of the literature.

sections.title: Methodological Approaches to Automated Literature Review Generation
sections.text: extractive_summarization; abstractive_summarization; retrieval_augmented_generation systems make claims including: The LLM-based approach (GPT-3.5-turbo) achieved the highest ROUGE-1 score of 0.364, outperforming T5 (0.268) and spaCy (0.257); All three NLP approaches (spaCy, T5, and GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125) can produce satisfactory results in automating literature review generation.

sections.title: Performance Evaluation and Metric Analysis
sections.text: Reported metrics include: ROUGE-1; ROUGE-2; ROUGE-L; ROUGE-Lsum; ROUGE-1; ROUGE-2; ROUGE-L; ROUGE-Lsum; ROUGE-1; ROUGE-2; ROUGE-L; ROUGE-Lsum; ROUGE-1; ROUGE-2; ROUGE-L; ROUGE-Lsum. Metric reporting practices vary considerably across papers, making direct cross-study comparison difficult in several cases.

sections.title: Datasets and Generalizability Constraints
sections.text: This section synthesizes findings from 1 papers in the review corpus, addressing text_summarization; multi_document_summarization; automated_literature_review_generation with extractive_summarization; abstractive_summarization; retrieval_augmented_generation. The comparative evidence matrix structures claims, metrics, and limitations to support structured analysis.

sections.title: Language and Geographic Representation Gaps
sections.text:
The following gaps have been verified through the evidence matrix:

Gap [cbf8c16a-90d2-498a-b3bc-974010b1ca55] (medium severity): Current coverage appears to underrepresent Chinese-language or Chinese-context studies.
Gap [9f897d4f-e5e3-437d-b2fd-cf8b0c3c162a] (medium severity): There are unresolved claim differences across papers under the same task, suggesting a need for clearer comparison criteria.

sections.title: Open Challenges and Future Research Directions
sections.text: This review has examined 1 papers covering text_summarization; multi_document_summarization; automated_literature_review_generation using extractive_summarization; abstractive_summarization; retrieval_augmented_generation. Key gaps identified include: [cbf8c16a-90d2-498a-b3bc-974010b1ca55] Current coverage appears to underrepresent Chinese-language or Chinese-context studies. [9f897d4f-e5e3-437d-b2fd-cf8b0c3c162a] There are unresolved claim differences across papers under the same task, suggesting a need for clearer comparison criteria.. Future research directions emerging from this analysis include addressing metric standardization, expanding linguistic coverage, and resolving conflicting performance claims through rigorous comparative evaluation.
